 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	<!doctype html>
     2	<html lang="en">
     3	<head>
     4	<meta charset="utf-8">
     5	<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
     6	<title>Commerce Truth Audit — whole-site redesign v2 (Oxblood system)</title>
     7	<style>
     8	  :root{
     9	    /* GALLERY WHITE — no grid. Paper is a premium off-white (#FBFBFD), not pure #FFF (pure white+black
    10	       is an AI-median tell — research 2026-07-08); ink is a warm near-black that harmonizes with wine. */
    11	    --paper:#FBFBFD; --panel:#F5F3F4; --panel-2:#EFEBEC;
    12	    --ink:#1A1712; --ink-2:#463F3C; --muted:#726A66;
    13	    --hairline:rgba(74,14,26,.10); --hairline-2:rgba(74,14,26,.17);
    14	    /* BRAND — burgundy/wine ramp (the ONE brand accent). Primary #8A2233 (~7:1 on paper, AA as fill
    15	       AND as text/icon on white); ladder from wine-900..500. */
    16	    --wine-900:#3E0A13; --wine-800:#55101D; --wine-700:#6E1423; --wine-600:#8A2233; --wine-500:#9E3143;
    17	    --accent:#8A2233; --accent-hi:#6E1423; --accent-soft:rgba(138,34,51,.07); --accent-line:rgba(138,34,51,.24);
    18	    /* SEVERITY — kept PERCEPTUALLY FAR from brand: error is brighter/warmer/more-orange, warn amber,
    19	       success a deep green (so burgundy reads BRAND, never alarm). Always paired with icon-shape + word. */
    20	    --error:#D92D20; --error-soft:rgba(217,45,32,.08);
    21	    --warn:#B54708; --warn-soft:rgba(181,71,8,.11);
    22	    --ok:#067647; --ok-soft:rgba(6,118,71,.09);
    23	    --pass:#067647;
    24	    --radius:8px; --radius-lg:14px;
    25	    --shadow-1:0 1px 2px rgba(74,14,26,.05);
    26	    --shadow-2:0 6px 22px rgba(74,14,26,.08),0 1px 2px rgba(74,14,26,.05);
    27	    --serif:ui-serif,"New York","Iowan Old Style","Palatino Linotype",Palatino,Georgia,"Times New Roman",serif;
    28	    --sans:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Inter","Segoe UI",Roboto,Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif;
    29	    --mono:"SFMono-Regular",ui-monospace,"JetBrains Mono",Menlo,monospace;
    30	    --col:1120px; --ease:cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1);
    31	  }
    32	  *{box-sizing:border-box}
    33	  html{-webkit-text-size-adjust:100%;scroll-behavior:smooth}
    34	  body{margin:0;color:var(--ink);background:var(--paper);font-family:var(--sans);line-height:1.55;
    35	    -webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased;text-rendering:optimizeLegibility;font-feature-settings:"tnum","ss01"}
    36	  a{color:inherit}
    37	  .serif{font-family:var(--serif)}
    38	  .mono{font-family:var(--mono);font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
    39	  .col{max-width:var(--col);margin:0 auto;padding:0 clamp(18px,4vw,36px)}
    40	  .ic{width:1.05em;height:1.05em;flex:none;vertical-align:-2px}
    41	
    42	  h1,h2,h3{font-family:var(--serif);font-weight:600;letter-spacing:-.012em;text-wrap:balance}
    43	  .eyebrow{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:7px;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11.5px;letter-spacing:.15em;
    44	    text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--accent);font-weight:600;margin:0 0 16px}
    45	  .eyebrow .ic{color:var(--accent)}
    46	  .kicker{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.13em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted);font-weight:600}
    47	
    48	  /* ============ NAV ============ */
    49	  .nav{position:sticky;top:0;z-index:40;background:rgba(255,255,255,.88);
    50	    backdrop-filter:saturate(1.4) blur(10px);-webkit-backdrop-filter:saturate(1.4) blur(10px);
    51	    border-bottom:1px solid var(--hairline)}
    52	  .nav-in{max-width:var(--col);margin:0 auto;display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;gap:4px;padding:11px clamp(18px,4vw,36px)}
    53	  .brand{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:9px;font-family:var(--serif);font-weight:600;letter-spacing:-.01em;
    54	    font-size:16px;margin-right:18px;text-decoration:none}
    55	  .brand .mk{width:22px;height:22px;color:var(--accent)}
    56	  .navlink{font-size:13px;color:var(--muted);text-decoration:none;padding:6px 11px;border-radius:7px;
    57	    transition:background .16s var(--ease),color .16s;white-space:nowrap}
    58	  .navlink:hover{background:var(--panel-2);color:var(--ink)}
    59	  .navlink[aria-current="page"]{background:var(--accent);color:#fff}
    60	  .navlink:focus-visible,.brand:focus-visible{outline:2px solid var(--accent);outline-offset:2px}
    61	  .status{margin-left:auto;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:7px;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;
    62	    letter-spacing:.04em;color:var(--muted);background:var(--panel-2);border:1px solid var(--hairline);border-radius:999px;padding:5px 11px}
    63	  .status .pip{width:6px;height:6px;border-radius:50%;background:var(--accent)}
    64	
    65	  /* ============ SHARED ============ */
    66	  .view{animation:vfade .28s var(--ease) both}
    67	  @keyframes vfade{from{opacity:0;transform:translateY(6px)}to{opacity:1;transform:none}}
    68	  .section{padding:clamp(46px,7vw,84px) 0;border-top:1px solid var(--hairline)}
    69	  .section:first-of-type{border-top:0}
    70	  .lede{font-size:clamp(15px,1.7vw,17px);color:var(--ink-2);max-width:62ch;text-wrap:pretty}
    71	  .lede+.lede{margin-top:8px}
    72	  .plainlead{color:var(--ink)} .techlead{color:var(--muted);font-size:14px}
    73	  h2.big{font-size:clamp(25px,3.8vw,40px);line-height:1.08;margin:0 0 16px;max-width:20ch}
    74	  .stack>*+*{margin-top:12px}
    75	
    76	  .reveal{opacity:0;transform:translateY(14px);transition:opacity .5s var(--ease),transform .5s var(--ease)}
    77	  .reveal.in{opacity:1;transform:none}
    78	
    79	  /* SIMULATED banner */
    80	  .sim{display:flex;gap:13px;align-items:flex-start;background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--hairline-2);
    81	    border-radius:var(--radius-lg);padding:13px 16px;margin:22px 0}
    82	  .sim-tag{flex:none;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;font-weight:600;
    83	    letter-spacing:.12em;color:#fff;background:var(--accent);padding:5px 10px;border-radius:7px}
    84	  .sim-text{font-size:12.5px;color:var(--ink-2);line-height:1.5}
    85	
    86	  /* buttons */
    87	  .btn{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;font-size:14px;font-weight:550;text-decoration:none;
    88	    padding:11px 18px;border-radius:var(--radius);border:1px solid transparent;cursor:pointer;min-height:44px;
    89	    transition:transform .16s var(--ease),background .16s,border-color .16s,box-shadow .16s}
    90	  .btn.primary{background:var(--accent);color:#fff;box-shadow:var(--shadow-1)}
    91	  .btn.primary:hover{transform:translateY(-1px);background:var(--accent-hi);box-shadow:var(--shadow-2)}
    92	  .btn.ghost{background:transparent;border-color:var(--hairline-2);color:var(--ink)}
    93	  .btn.ghost:hover{background:var(--panel);border-color:var(--accent-line)}
    94	  .btn:focus-visible{outline:2px solid var(--accent);outline-offset:2px}
    95	  .btn .ic{transition:transform .16s var(--ease)} .btn:hover .ic{transform:translateX(2px)}
    96	
    97	  /* stat rail */
    98	  .stats{display:grid;gap:12px}
    99	  .stat{border:1px solid var(--hairline);border-radius:var(--radius-lg);padding:16px;background:var(--paper);transition:border-color .16s,box-shadow .16s}
   100	  .stat:hover{border-color:var(--accent-line);box-shadow:var(--shadow-1)}
   101	  .stat .v{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:30px;font-weight:600;letter-spacing:-.02em;line-height:1;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
   102	  .stat .l{margin-top:9px;font-size:12px;font-weight:550;color:var(--muted)}
   103	  .stat .s{margin-top:3px;font-size:11px;color:var(--muted)}
   104	  .stat.accent{border-color:var(--accent-line);background:var(--accent-soft)} .stat.accent .v{color:var(--accent)}
   105	
   106	  /* tables */
   107	  .tbl-wrap{border:1px solid var(--hairline);border-radius:var(--radius-lg);overflow:hidden;overflow-x:auto}
   108	  table{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13.5px}
   109	  thead th{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10.5px;letter-spacing:.09em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted);
   110	    font-weight:600;text-align:left;padding:12px 16px;background:var(--panel);border-bottom:1px solid var(--hairline)}
   111	  tbody td{padding:12px 16px;border-top:1px solid var(--hairline)}
   112	  tbody tr{transition:background .14s} tbody tr:hover{background:var(--panel)}
   113	  td .mlink{color:var(--ink);font-weight:550;text-decoration:none;border-bottom:1px solid var(--accent-line)}
   114	  td .mlink:hover{border-color:var(--accent)}
   115	
   116	  /* chips */
   117	  .chip{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10.5px;font-weight:600;
   118	    letter-spacing:.04em;padding:4px 9px;border-radius:999px;white-space:nowrap;text-transform:uppercase}
   119	  .chip .ic{width:12px;height:12px}
   120	  .chip.pass,.chip.low,.chip.sent{color:var(--ok);background:var(--ok-soft)}
   121	  .chip.warn,.chip.medium,.chip.held,.chip.drafted{color:var(--warn);background:var(--warn-soft)}
   122	  .chip.error,.chip.high,.chip.rejected,.chip.blocked{color:var(--error);background:var(--error-soft)}
   123	
   124	  /* callout */
   125	  .note{border:1px solid var(--hairline);background:var(--panel);border-radius:var(--radius);padding:11px 14px;font-size:13px;color:var(--ink-2)}
   126	  .note.warnish{border-color:rgba(162,105,28,.3);background:var(--warn-soft);color:var(--warn)}
   127	  .panel{border:1px solid var(--hairline);border-radius:var(--radius-lg);padding:20px;background:var(--paper);transition:border-color .16s,box-shadow .16s,transform .16s var(--ease)}
   128	  .panel:hover{border-color:var(--accent-line);box-shadow:var(--shadow-2);transform:translateY(-2px)}
   129	  .grid2{display:grid;gap:14px} @media(min-width:720px){.grid2{grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr}}
   130	  .grid3{display:grid;gap:12px} @media(min-width:720px){.grid3{grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr)}}
   131	  .icon-badge{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:34px;height:34px;border-radius:9px;
   132	    background:var(--accent-soft);color:var(--accent);margin-bottom:12px}
   133	  .icon-badge .ic{width:18px;height:18px}
   134	
   135	  /* ============ REPORT (document) ============ */
   136	  .rpt-verdict{display:flex;align-items:baseline;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:10px 20px;padding-bottom:24px;border-bottom:1px solid var(--hairline)}
   137	  .rpt-flag{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:7px;font-family:var(--mono);font-weight:700;font-size:13px;letter-spacing:.12em;
   138	    padding:6px 12px;border-radius:7px;color:var(--error);border:1.5px solid var(--error)}
   139	  .rpt-num{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(40px,7vw,58px);font-weight:600;letter-spacing:-.02em;line-height:1;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
   140	  .rpt-tally{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:14px;color:var(--muted)} .rpt-tally b{color:var(--ink)}
   141	  .rail{display:grid;grid-template-columns:130px 1fr;gap:24px;padding:28px 0;border-top:1px solid var(--hairline)}
   142	  .rail>h3{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted);margin:0;font-weight:600;font-family:var(--mono)}
   143	  .meta-row{display:grid;grid-template-columns:130px 1fr;gap:14px;padding:9px 0;border-bottom:1px solid var(--hairline);font-size:13.5px}
   144	  .meta-row dt{color:var(--muted);margin:0} .meta-row dd{margin:0;color:var(--ink-2)}
   145	  ol.findings{list-style:none;margin:0;padding:0}
   146	  .finding{padding:26px 0;border-top:1px solid var(--hairline)}
   147	  .finding:first-child{border-top:0;padding-top:0}
   148	  .f-lead{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr auto;gap:16px;align-items:start}
   149	  .f-plain{font-family:var(--serif);font-size:clamp(16px,2vw,19px);line-height:1.4;margin:0;text-wrap:pretty}
   150	  .sev{flex:none;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10.5px;font-weight:600;
   151	    letter-spacing:.06em;text-transform:uppercase;padding:4px 9px;border-radius:6px;white-space:nowrap}
   152	  .sev .ic{width:12px;height:12px}
   153	  .sev.error{color:var(--error);background:var(--error-soft)} .sev.warn{color:var(--warn);background:var(--warn-soft)}
   154	  .receipts{margin-top:16px;display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(2,1fr);gap:1px;background:var(--hairline);
   155	    border:1px solid var(--hairline);border-radius:11px;overflow:hidden}
   156	  .rc{background:var(--paper);padding:11px 14px}
   157	  .rc dt{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10px;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted);margin:0 0 4px}
   158	  .rc dd{margin:0;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:12px;color:var(--ink);word-break:break-word;line-height:1.4}
   159	  .rc dd .sub{display:block;color:var(--muted);margin-top:2px;font-size:11px}
   160	  .toggle{display:inline-flex;background:var(--panel-2);border-radius:9px;padding:3px;gap:2px;border:1px solid var(--hairline)}
   161	  .toggle button{font:550 12.5px var(--sans);color:var(--muted);background:transparent;border:0;border-radius:7px;padding:7px 13px;cursor:pointer}
   162	  .toggle button[aria-selected="true"]{background:var(--paper);color:var(--ink);box-shadow:var(--shadow-1)}
   163	  .toggle button:focus-visible{outline:2px solid var(--accent);outline-offset:2px}
   164	
   165	  /* landing hero */
   166	  .hero{display:grid;gap:40px;align-items:center}
   167	  @media(min-width:920px){.hero{grid-template-columns:1.05fr .95fr}}
   168	  .hero h1{font-size:clamp(34px,5.6vw,60px);line-height:1.02;margin:0 0 20px;letter-spacing:-.02em}
   169	  .hero .lede{font-size:clamp(16px,2vw,19px)}
   170	  .hero-cta{display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:12px;margin-top:28px;align-items:center}
   171	
   172	  /* catch panel */
   173	  .catch{border:1px solid var(--hairline-2);border-radius:var(--radius-lg);overflow:hidden;background:var(--paper);box-shadow:var(--shadow-2)}
   174	  .catch-cap{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;padding:12px 16px;border-bottom:1px solid var(--hairline);
   175	    font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.04em;color:var(--muted)}
   176	  .catch-body{padding:16px}
   177	  .record{border:1px solid var(--hairline);border-radius:var(--radius);background:var(--panel);padding:12px 14px;margin-bottom:14px}
   178	  .record .rl{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10.5px;letter-spacing:.05em;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;margin-bottom:8px}
   179	  .record .rf{display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:6px}
   180	  .rf .f{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11.5px;background:var(--paper);border:1px solid var(--hairline);border-radius:5px;padding:3px 8px;color:var(--ink-2)}
   181	  .claim{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr auto;gap:10px;align-items:center;padding:11px 0;border-top:1px solid var(--hairline)}
   182	  .claim .ct{font-size:13.5px} .claim .cv{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:11px;color:var(--muted);margin-top:2px;display:block}
   183	  .verdict-tag{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10px;font-weight:600;letter-spacing:.04em;
   184	    text-transform:uppercase;padding:4px 8px;border-radius:6px;white-space:nowrap}
   185	  .verdict-tag .ic{width:12px;height:12px}
   186	  .verdict-tag.ok{color:var(--ok);background:var(--ok-soft)} .verdict-tag.no{color:var(--error);background:var(--error-soft)}
   187	  .held{margin-top:14px;border:1px solid var(--accent-line);background:var(--accent-soft);border-radius:var(--radius);padding:12px 14px}
   188	  .held .h{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:7px;font-weight:600;font-size:13.5px;color:var(--accent);font-family:var(--serif)}
   189	  .held .p{font-size:12.5px;color:var(--ink-2);margin-top:4px}
   190	  .decide{margin-top:12px} .decide .dn{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10.5px;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;margin-bottom:8px}
   191	  .decide-btns{display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:8px}
   192	  .dbtn{font-size:12.5px;padding:8px 12px;border-radius:7px;border:1px solid var(--hairline-2);background:var(--paper);color:var(--muted)}
   193	
   194	  /* flow (no numbers) */
   195	  .flow{display:grid;gap:12px}@media(min-width:760px){.flow{grid-template-columns:repeat(5,1fr)}}
   196	  .step{border:1px solid var(--hairline);border-radius:var(--radius-lg);padding:16px;background:var(--paper);transition:border-color .16s,transform .16s var(--ease)}
   197	  .step:hover{border-color:var(--accent-line);transform:translateY(-2px)}
   198	  .step.gate{border-color:var(--accent-line);background:var(--accent-soft)}
   199	  .step .si{width:26px;height:26px;color:var(--accent);margin-bottom:10px}
   200	  .step .sl{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10px;letter-spacing:.07em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted);margin-bottom:6px}
   201	  .step.gate .sl{color:var(--accent)}
   202	  .step h4{margin:0 0 6px;font-size:14px;font-family:var(--serif)}
   203	  .step p{margin:0;font-size:12.5px;color:var(--muted);line-height:1.45}
   204	
   205	  /* bars */
   206	  .bar-row{margin-bottom:12px}
   207	  .bar-top{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:13px;margin-bottom:5px}
   208	  .bar-top .bv{font-family:var(--mono);color:var(--muted);font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
   209	  .bar{height:8px;border-radius:5px;background:var(--panel-2);overflow:hidden}
   210	  .bar>i{display:block;height:100%;background:var(--ink);border-radius:5px;transition:width .8s var(--ease)}
   211	  .bar.acc>i{background:var(--accent)}
   212	
   213	  /* footer */
   214	  footer.site{border-top:1px solid var(--hairline);margin-top:20px;background:var(--panel)}
   215	  footer.site .col{padding-top:28px;padding-bottom:40px}
   216	  footer.site p{font-size:12.5px;color:var(--muted);line-height:1.6;max-width:82ch;margin:0 0 8px}
   217	  .mockup-tag{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:7px;font-family:var(--mono);font-size:10.5px;letter-spacing:.05em;color:var(--muted);
   218	    border:1px dashed var(--hairline-2);border-radius:6px;padding:4px 10px;margin-bottom:12px}
   219	
   220	  @media(max-width:600px){.receipts{grid-template-columns:1fr}.rail{grid-template-columns:1fr;gap:10px}.meta-row{grid-template-columns:1fr}}
   221	  @media (prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){*{animation:none!important;transition:none!important;scroll-behavior:auto}.reveal{opacity:1;transform:none}}
   222	</style>
   223	</head>

REPLAY over fictional display names + synthetic activation state (the adapter ingests real public DataSF records; the demo shows invented ones) — not production logs, real sends, real marketplace access, or real-impact data. The "real Gemini" output shown is a recorded static fixture (reproduce it locally with your own key). Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, DataSF, or any named business.</p>

   246	  </div>

codex
The mockup confirms the nav link font is inherited sans at 13px, so the mono-to-sans fix matches the spec. I’m checking remaining app metadata and page surfaces now, because title templating and remapped root tokens can regress routes outside these three files.

{
  "name": "commerce-truth-audit",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "description": "The truth layer for agentic commerce: a deterministic verifier of what platforms and AI-agent surfaces claim (menus, prices, availability) vs the merchant system-of-record, UCP/ACP conformance checking, and an NYC §20-563.3 fee-cap audit. Simulated corpus; prototype. Lineage: the ActivationOps AI activation module is archived runnable under legacy/.",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "eslint . --max-warnings=0",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:legacy": "vitest run legacy/activation/evals",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "coverage": "vitest run --coverage",
    "verify": "npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test && npm run build",
    "verify:full": "npm run verify && npm run test:e2e",
    "check:fixtures": "node bin/check.mjs check fixtures/synthetic-restaurant/acp-feed.drifted.json --against fixtures/synthetic-restaurant/sor.catalog.json",
    "check:fixtures:clean": "node bin/check.mjs check fixtures/synthetic-restaurant/acp-feed.faithful.json --against fixtures/synthetic-restaurant/sor.catalog.json",
    "check:conformance": "node bin/check.mjs check fixtures/ucp-conformance-ci/valid/search-full-catalog.json --conformance",
    "check:fees": "node bin/check.mjs fees fixtures/synthetic-restaurant/fees/statement.drifted.json",
    "check:fees:clean": "node bin/check.mjs fees fixtures/synthetic-restaurant/fees/statement.faithful.json",
    "demo": "node bin/check.mjs demo",
    "mcp": "node bin/mcp-server.mjs",
    "mcp:record-transcript": "node evals/mcp/record-transcript.mjs",
    "fixtures:wedge": "node scripts-ts/generate-wedge-fixtures.mts",
    "fixtures:fees": "node scripts-ts/generate-fee-fixtures.mts",
    "fixtures:ucp": "node scripts-ts/generate-ucp-conformance-corpus.mts",

  },

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' AGENTS.md" in /Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine
 succeeded in 0ms:
# Agent Instructions

`RULES.md` is the source of truth for this project. If anything here conflicts with `RULES.md`, **`RULES.md` wins.** This file is a short entry point for any agent (Claude Code account 1/2, Claude CLI, Codex) or human working in the repo.

## Start here

1. Read `RULES.md`, `PROJECT_STATE.md`, `CURRENT_TASK.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `docs/task-log.md`.
2. Run `git status`.
3. Summarize current phase, active task, changed files, unfinished work, risks, and the next safest step — then wait for human approval before continuing.

## How the project runs

The project is **human-led and AI-assisted**. Work proceeds as small, reviewed slices in flow state — not as a one-time review pass. Claude Code plans and builds; Codex reviews adversarially, reviews changed files, rescues stalled work, and audits before shipping; the human owner makes the final calls. See `docs/dual-model-workflow.md`.

Match the workflow weight to the risk (`RULES.md` §13): a lightweight loop for deterministic/offline slices, the full Claude + Codex loop for integrations, live sends, auth/security, data writes, and publishing.

## Ground rules (summary — full set in `RULES.md`)

- Treat all merchant data as dummy/simulated. No real DoorDash access, data, or business impact.
- No secrets anywhere (`RULES.md` §11): no API keys, tokens, credentials, or personal data in code, CSVs, logs, screenshots, commits, docs, or prompts.
- No live Supabase, n8n, Slack, Resend, or Gemini integration until the offline thin slice is complete and reviewed.
- Prefer simple, auditable workflow design over broad automation.
- Deterministic logic before AI; structured outputs before prose; human approval before risky automation.
- Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed. Never claim "no AI was used" or "AI built this."
- Verify platform claims against current docs or the installed tool, or mark them UNVERIFIED (`RULES.md` §6).

## Where things get recorded

See `RULES.md` §10. In short: small edits → `docs/task-log.md`; meaningful work → `docs/implementation-journal.md`; scope/architecture → `docs/decision-log.md`; active task → `CURRENT_TASK.md`; handoff → `HANDOFF.md`; state → `PROJECT_STATE.md`. Run `docs/checklists/prevent-repeat-checklist.md` before closing a task that hit a problem.

## Validation before claiming done

Re-check the file tree, confirm the definition of done (`RULES.md` §9), and confirm no live integrations or secrets were introduced.

 succeeded in 0ms:
import Link from "next/link";
import { CatchPanel } from "@/components/landing/CatchPanel";
import { Reveal } from "@/components/landing/Reveal";

export const metadata = {
  title: "ActivationOps — Merchant Activation review, with the facts checked",
  description:
    "A governed activation review surface for Merchant Operations: AI drafts merchant outreach, every claim is checked against that merchant's own record, and nothing false reaches a merchant without a person signing off. Simulated prototype; not affiliated with any marketplace.",
};

/** Small inline glyphs — no icon library on the landing, no emoji. */
function ArrowRight({ stroke = "#fff" }: { stroke?: string }) {
  return (
    <svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" aria-hidden="true">
      <path
        d="M3 8h9M9 4.5L12.5 8 9 11.5"
        fill="none"
        stroke={stroke}
        strokeWidth="1.6"
        strokeLinecap="round"
        strokeLinejoin="round"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}
function Chevron() {
  return (
    <svg className="chev" viewBox="0 0 13 13" fill="none" aria-hidden="true">
      <path
        d="M4.5 2.5L8.5 6.5L4.5 10.5"
        stroke="currentColor"
        strokeWidth="1.5"
        strokeLinecap="round"
        strokeLinejoin="round"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

const PIPELINE: Array<{ n: string; title: string; plain: string; gate?: boolean }> = [
  {
    n: "01",
    title: "Your data",
    plain: "The merchant's own record is the only thing a message is allowed to be true against.",
  },
  {
    n: "02",
    title: "AI drafts from it",
    plain:
      "The AI writes the outreach using that record — not the open web, and not the merchant's own typed-in text.",
  },
  {
    n: "03",
    title: "An exact automatic check",
    plain:
      "Every fact the message states has to match a field in the record, exactly. Anything that doesn't is flagged.",
    gate: true,
  },
  {
    n: "04",
    title: "An independent second reviewer",
    plain:
      "A separate AI reviewer re-reads the message and flags anything the data can't back — including facts slipped in casually.",
    gate: true,
  },
  {
    n: "05",
    title: "A person approves",
    plain:
      "Your reviewer approves, edits, or holds. Nothing sends itself, and every decision lands on the audit trail.",
  },
];

function BulletOpen() {
  return (
    <svg className="gl" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" aria-hidden="true">
      <circle cx="8" cy="8" r="6.4" fill="none" stroke="#9a9aa3" strokeWidth="1.4" />
    </svg>
  );
}
function BulletFilled() {
  return (
    <svg className="gl" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" aria-hidden="true">
      <circle cx="8" cy="8" r="7" fill="#0a0a0c" />
      <path
        d="M4.6 8.2l2.3 2.3L11.4 5.8"
        fill="none"
        stroke="#fff"
        strokeWidth="1.6"
        strokeLinecap="round"
        strokeLinejoin="round"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

export default function Landing() {
  return (
    <main>
      {/* ===== BEAT 1 — HERO · outcome/gain first, the FIXED honest headline ===== */}
      <section className="ds-wrap lp-hero" aria-labelledby="hero-h1">
        <div className="lp-hero-grid">
          <div>
            <p className="ds-eyebrow">
              <span className="idx">A</span> Merchant Activation &middot; review &amp; approval
            </p>
            <h1 id="hero-h1">
              AI writes your merchant outreach &mdash; and nothing reaches a merchant until every
              claim is checked against <span className="mark-word">their own data</span>.
            </h1>
            <p className="sub">
              Each message is checked against <em>that merchant&rsquo;s own record</em>{" "}
              before it can be sent. A confident-sounding claim that the data doesn&rsquo;t back is
              held for a person &mdash; never sent on its own.
            </p>
            <div className="lp-hero-cta">
              <Link className="ds-cta" href="/console">
                See it run on the console
                <ArrowRight />
              </Link>
              <a className="lp-howlink" href="#how">
                <svg width="13" height="13" viewBox="0 0 13 13" fill="none" aria-hidden="true">
                  <circle cx="6.5" cy="6.5" r="5.4" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1.2" />
                  <path
                    d="M6.5 5.6v3.2M6.5 4.1v.1"
                    stroke="currentColor"
                    strokeWidth="1.4"
                    strokeLinecap="round"
                  />
                </svg>
                How the checking works
              </a>
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* supporting visual: the SHOWN catch — settled in SSR, replays only when motion is on */}
          <aside className="lp-hero-aside" aria-label="Example of a checked outreach draft, held for approval">
            <CatchPanel />
          </aside>
        </div>
      </section>

      {/* ===== BEAT 2 — TRUST ANCHOR · honest credibility, early. No logo wall. ===== */}
      <section className="ds-wrap ds-section" id="trust" aria-labelledby="trust-h2">
        <Reveal>
          <p className="ds-eyebrow">
            <span className="idx">B</span> How a message earns the right to send
          </p>
          <h2 id="trust-h2" className="ds-h2 ds-h2big" style={{ maxWidth: "18ch" }}>
            Three checks stand between the AI and a merchant.
          </h2>
        </Reveal>
        <Reveal>
          <div className="lp-anchor">
            <div className="lp-anchor-grid">
              <div className="lp-astep">
                <span className="an">
                  <span className="dot" />
                  Step one
                </span>
                <h3 className="ds-h3">An exact, automatic check</h3>
                <p>
                  Every fact the message states is matched, exactly, against the merchant&rsquo;s
                  record. No match, no pass.
                </p>
              </div>
              <div className="lp-astep">
                <span className="an">
                  <span className="dot" />
                  Step two
                </span>
                <h3 className="ds-h3">An independent second reviewer</h3>
                <p>
                  A separate AI reviewer re-reads the message looking for anything the record
                  can&rsquo;t back &mdash; it doesn&rsquo;t grade its own work.
                </p>
              </div>
              <div className="lp-astep">
                <span className="an signal">
                  <span className="dot" />

   255	          <div style={{ marginTop: "var(--s6)", maxWidth: 560 }}>

   259	        <Reveal>
   260	          <p className="lp-anchor-foot" style={{ marginTop: 24 }}>
   261	            The same check runs on every draft in the queue. See it on a real example &mdash;{" "}
   262	            <Link
   263	              href="/console"
   264	              style={{
   265	                color: "var(--signal-text)",

   274	          <details className="ds-tech">
   275	            <summary>
   276	              <Chevron /> For technical reviewers &middot; what &ldquo;checked&rdquo; means here
   277	            </summary>
   278	            <div className="tech-body">
   279	              <p>
   280	                Each declared claim is verified by per-claim <em>entailment</em> against the
   281	                merchant&rsquo;s claimable fields, forward (claim&nbsp;&rarr;&nbsp;field): a
   282	                supported claim cites its backing field; an unsupported one is held. A
   283	                confident-sounding sentence with no field behind it &mdash; the go-live date &mdash;
   284	                has nothing to entail against, so it fails.
   285	              </p>
   286	              <p>
   287	                A second, <em>cross-family</em> reviewer then reads the prose in reverse
   288	                (prose&nbsp;&rarr;&nbsp;data) to catch facts asserted in passing but never declared
   289	                as claims. It runs on a different model family so the verifier isn&rsquo;t grading
   290	                its own family&rsquo;s output &mdash; maker&nbsp;&ne;&nbsp;judge at the model layer.
   291	              </p>
   292	              <p className="mono-line">
   293	                <span className="k">gate:</span> deterministic forward claim&rarr;field,
   294	                byte-reproducible, eval-locked &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; <span className="k">judge:</span>{" "}
   295	                cross-family entailment, reverse prose&rarr;data, recall-favoring

   302	      {/* ===== BEAT 5 — HOW IT WORKS · plain on top, technical opt-in ===== */}
   303	      <section className="ds-wrap ds-section" id="how" aria-labelledby="how-h2">

   305	          <p className="ds-eyebrow">
   306	            <span className="idx">E</span> How it works
   307	          </p>
   308	          <h2 id="how-h2" className="ds-h2 ds-h2big" style={{ maxWidth: "20ch" }}>
   309	            The AI is checked, not trusted.
   310	          </h2>
   311	          <p className="ds-lede" style={{ marginTop: 20 }}>
   312	            Five stages. Cheap, exact checks run before the slower ones; an independent reviewer
   313	            runs near the end; a person always has the last word.

   315	        </Reveal>
   316	        <Reveal stagger>

   321	                <div className="pmain">
   322	                  <div className="ptop">
   323	                    <h3 className="ds-h3">{p.title}</h3>

   332	        <Reveal>
   333	          <details className="ds-tech">
   334	            <summary>
   335	              <Chevron /> For technical reviewers &middot; the real pipeline
   336	            </summary>
   337	            <div className="tech-body">
   338	              <p>
   339	                Stage 3 is a <em>deterministic</em> forward gate: declared claims are checked
   340	                claim&nbsp;&rarr;&nbsp;field and the output is byte-for-byte reproducible &mdash; the
   341	                fast, exact floor, pinned and eval-locked against a golden differential oracle. The
   342	                merchant&rsquo;s untrusted name never enters the model prompt (an injection cut), so
   343	                a hostile name can&rsquo;t steer the draft.
   344	              </p>
   345	              <p>
   346	                Stage 4 is a <em>cross-family entailment</em> judge that closes the gap from the
   347	                other direction (prose&nbsp;&rarr;&nbsp;data) &mdash; catching facts asserted in
   348	                prose but never declared as claims. Running it on a different model family keeps
   349	                maker&nbsp;&ne;&nbsp;judge at the model layer; it&rsquo;s tuned recall-favoring,
   350	                because a false hold costs a glance and a missed fabrication costs trust.
   351	              </p>
   352	            </div>
   353	          </details>
   354	        </Reveal>
   355	      </section>
   356	
   357	      {/* ===== BEAT 6 — DIFFERENTIATION · plain ===== */}
   358	      <section className="ds-wrap ds-section" id="vs" aria-labelledby="vs-h2">
   359	        <Reveal>
   360	          <p className="ds-eyebrow">
   361	            <span className="idx">F</span> What makes it different
   362	          </p>
   363	          <h2 id="vs-h2" className="ds-h2 ds-h2big" style={{ maxWidth: "18ch" }}>
   364	            A safety filter checks the message. We check the facts.
   365	          </h2>
   366	        </Reveal>
   367	        <Reveal>
   368	          <div className="lp-compare">
   369	            <div className="lp-ccol them">
   370	              <div className="crole">an ordinary AI safety filter (&ldquo;guardrail&rdquo;)</div>
   371	              <h3 className="ds-h3">Is this message appropriate?</h3>
   372	              <ul>
   373	                <li>
   374	                  <BulletOpen /> Tone, rudeness, off-policy language

   376	                <li>

   380	                  <BulletOpen /> Scans for leaked personal data

   382	              </ul>

   384	            </div>
   385	            <div className="lp-ccol ours">
   386	              <div className="crole">ActivationOps</div>
   387	              <h3 className="ds-h3">Is this message true for this merchant?</h3>
   388	              <ul>
   389	                <li>
   390	                  <BulletFilled /> Every claim checked against the data row
   391	                </li>
   392	                <li>
   393	                  <BulletFilled /> An exact check, then an independent reviewer

   395	                <li>
   396	                  <BulletFilled /> Evidence on every line, held for a person
   397	                </li>
   398	              </ul>
   399	              <p className="cq">
   400	                Reads the message <em>against the record</em>.
   401	              </p>
   402	            </div>
   403	          </div>
   404	        </Reveal>
   405	      </section>
   406	
   407	      {/* ===== BEAT 7 — OBJECTION · "is the reviewer right?" — METHOD ONLY ===== */}
   408	      <section className="ds-wrap ds-section" id="reviewer" aria-labelledby="rev-h2">
   409	        <Reveal>
   410	          <p className="ds-eyebrow">
   411	            <span className="idx">G</span> The obvious question
   412	          </p>
   413	          <h2 id="rev-h2" className="ds-h2 ds-h2big" style={{ maxWidth: "20ch" }}>
   414	            &ldquo;How do you know the AI reviewer is right?&rdquo;
   415	          </h2>
   416	          <p className="ds-lede" style={{ marginTop: 20 }}>
   417	            Fair question &mdash; so we don&rsquo;t ask you to assume it.{" "}
   418	            <em>We check the reviewer against people.</em>
   419	          </p>
   420	        </Reveal>
   421	        <Reveal stagger>
   422	          <div className="lp-qa">
   423	            <div className="lp-qrow">
   424	              <div className="qh">We measure it against human judgment.</div>
   425	              <div className="qb">
   426	                Human reviewers label a set of real examples by hand, then we measure how often the
   427	                AI reviewer agrees with them &mdash; and tune it to catch more rather than miss.
   428	                <div className="lp-pending">
   429	                  <span className="ring" />
   430	                  calibration run in progress &middot; figures published when it clears the bar
   431	                </div>
   432	              </div>
   433	            </div>
   434	            <div className="lp-qrow">
   435	              <div className="qh">It&rsquo;s tuned to hold, not to over-block.</div>
   436	              <div className="qb">
   437	                When it&rsquo;s unsure, it holds for a person &mdash; it never auto-rejects.{" "}
   438	                <em>A false hold costs a glance; a shipped falsehood costs trust.</em> So we accept a
   439	                few extra holds to avoid a single thing slipping through.
   440	              </div>
   441	            </div>
   442	            <div className="lp-qrow">
   443	              <div className="qh">The exact check underneath is locked.</div>
   444	              <div className="qb">
   445	                The automatic, exact check is pinned &mdash; it produces the same result every time
   446	                on the same input, so the floor the whole tool stands on can&rsquo;t quietly drift.
   447	              </div>
   448	            </div>
   449	          </div>
   450	        </Reveal>
   451	        <Reveal>
   452	          <details className="ds-tech">
   453	            <summary>
   454	              <Chevron /> For technical reviewers &middot; what we measure
   455	            </summary>
   456	            <div className="tech-body">
   457	              <p>
   458	                The judge is calibrated on a labeled gold set: <em>recall</em> on the gate-passing
   459	                subset is the primary objective, with the <em>precision</em> cost reported at the
   460	                operating point, on held-out data &mdash; recall-favoring by design.
   461	              </p>
   462	              <p className="mono-line">
   463	                <span className="k">reported:</span> precision / recall / F1 &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;
   464	                inter-rater agreement (Cohen&rsquo;s &kappa;) &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; test-retest
   465	                flip-rate &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; held-out
   466	              </p>
   467	              <p>
   468	                These are the metrics the calibration report will carry. Figures appear only once
   469	                the run completes and clears the acceptance bar &mdash; until then, the method
   470	                stands; the numbers are pending, not assumed.
   471	              </p>
   472	            </div>
   473	          </details>
   474	        </Reveal>
   475	      </section>
   476	
   477	      {/* ===== BEAT 8 — CLOSE · "See it run" → /console ===== */}
   478	      <section className="ds-wrap ds-section" id="run" aria-labelledby="run-h2">
   479	        <Reveal>
   480	          <p className="ds-eyebrow">
   481	            <span className="idx">H</span> See it run
   482	          </p>
   483	          <h2 id="run-h2" className="ds-h2 ds-h2big" style={{ maxWidth: "20ch" }}>
   484	            Watch one draft get checked, line by line.
   485	          </h2>
   486	          <p className="ds-lede" style={{ marginTop: 20 }}>
   487	            A recorded walkthrough on a single onboarding draft &mdash; the same checks, the same
   488	            hold, the same audit entry. On your data, the records and drafts would be yours.
   489	          </p>
   490	          <div className="lp-cta-wrap">
   491	            <Link className="ds-cta" href="/console">
   492	              See it run on the console
   493	              <ArrowRight />
   494	            </Link>
   495	            <span className="lp-cta-note">a recorded, replayable run &mdash; not a sign-up</span>
   496	          </div>
   497	        </Reveal>
   498	      </section>
   499	
   500	      {/* ===== BEAT 9 — HONESTY (in-page; the global contentinfo footer lives in layout) ===== */}
   501	      <section className="ds-wrap ds-section" id="honesty" aria-label="Honesty disclosure">
   502	        <p style={{ fontSize: 13.5, lineHeight: 1.62, color: "var(--body)", maxWidth: "76ch" }}>
   503	          A simulated prototype on de-identified, public open data. Merchant and reviewer names are
   504	          fictional. Not affiliated with DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, or any marketplace. The
   505	          walkthrough is a recorded, replayable run &mdash; labeled, not live &mdash; and accuracy
   506	          figures are pending until the calibration run completes. Human-led, AI-assisted,
   507	          professionally reviewed.
   508	        </p>
   509	        <p
   510	          className="ds-mono"
   511	          style={{
   512	            marginTop: 18,
   513	            paddingTop: 16,
   514	            borderTop: "1px solid var(--rule)",
   515	            fontSize: 11,
   516	            color: "var(--faint)",
   517	            letterSpacing: "0.005em",
   518	            lineHeight: 1.8,
   519	          }}
   520	        >
   521	          SIMULATED &middot; FICTIONAL NAMES &middot; NO REAL MERCHANT DATA &middot; NOT AFFILIATED
   522	          WITH ANY MARKETPLACE &middot; REPLAY / RECORDED &middot; FIGURES PENDING CALIBRATION
   523	          &middot; HUMAN-LED, AI-ASSISTED, PROFESSIONALLY REVIEWED
   524	        </p>
   525	      </section>
   526	    </main>
   527	  );
   528	}

codex
There is one serious cross-file interaction emerging: the root layout title template does not replace child page titles, it wraps them. The existing landing page metadata still says “ActivationOps,” so the new template would surface mixed identity unless that page is included in the same slice or the root template avoids wrapping stale titles.
exec

diff --git a/app/globals.css b/app/globals.css
index ed7972c..7932896 100644
--- a/app/globals.css
+++ b/app/globals.css
@@ -1,40 +1,55 @@
 @import "tailwindcss";
 
 /* ============================================================================
-   ActivationOps — "Ledger Enterprise" design system (Stage 1 foundation)
-   Ported from mockups/v3-ledger-enterprise.html. Premium white ground, a single
-   vermilion accent (two lightnesses of one hue), cool-neutral ink/graphite, and
-   one signature primitive: the hairline-ruled ledger row (field · value · verdict).
-   Fonts are self-hosted via next/font/google (see app/layout.tsx) and reach this
-   file as CSS variables (--font-sans / --font-mono / --font-geist-mono).
+   Curbside Commons — "Oxblood" site design system (S1 foundation, 2026-07-08)
+   Spec: mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/whole-site-gallery-v2.html (owner-picked).
+
+   RETHEME-IN-PLACE: every :root token NAME below is preserved (layout.tsx, Nav,
+   and the landing consume them) but the VALUES are remapped to Oxblood — a premium
+   off-white ground (#FBFBFD), a warm near-black ink ramp (harmonizing with the
+   accent, not cool-gray), wine-tinted hairlines, and ONE burgundy accent (#8A2233,
+   two lightnesses of one hue). An ADDITIVE "OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1)" section holds
+   the site-scoped tokens (wine ramp / severity / serif / ease) and the shared shell
+   primitives (nav + footer). Fonts still self-host via next/font/google (see
+   app/layout.tsx) as --font-sans / --font-mono / --font-geist-mono.
+
+   INTENTIONALLY UNTOUCHED (byte-for-byte, superseded by later slices):
+   • the entire `.rpt-wrap` report/demo Ledger block (line ~1105 → its print block)
+     — it redefines --paper/--ink/--body/--graphite/--hairline/--um/--sev-* locally,
+     so it is insulated from these :root remaps (S3 re-skins it to Oxblood);
+   • all `.ds-*` / `.cp-*` / `.lp-*` landing rule blocks — they inherit the new token
+     VALUES (new colors, old layout = the expected hybrid until S2 supersedes them).
    ========================================================================== */
 
 :root {
   color-scheme: light;
 
-  /* ground — true premium white, no warmth */
-  --bg: #ffffff;
-  --bg-2: #fcfcfd;
-  --bg-3: #f7f7f9;
-
-  /* ink + graphite structure (cool neutrals, AA+ on white) */
-  --ink: #0a0a0c;
-  --ink-2: #16161a;
-  --body: #34343c; /* 11.6:1 — body text */
-  --graphite: #52525c; /* 7.72:1 — labels, values */
-  --muted: #5d5d68; /* 6.50:1 — secondary mono */
-  --faint: #6c6c78; /* 5.16:1 — non-essential ≥13px */
-
-  /* hairlines */
-  --rule: #ececef;
-  --rule-2: #e0e0e4;
-  --rule-3: #d2d2d8;
-
-  /* THE single accent — vermilion, two lightnesses of one hue */
-  --signal: #e2402a; /* fills, dots, rules, marks */
-  --signal-text: #c0301b; /* 5.71:1 on white — small labels / verdicts */
-  --signal-tint: #fbedea; /* faint held-row ground */
-  --signal-rule: #f0d6cf; /* hairline at signal hue */
+  /* ground — premium off-white (NOT pure #fff; pure white+black is an AI-median
+     tell). Warm-neutral steps down. */
+  --bg: #fbfbfd;
+  --bg-2: #f8f7f8;
+  --bg-3: #f5f3f4;
+
+  /* ink + graphite structure — a WARM near-black ramp (R>G>B), harmonizing with
+     the wine accent. Contrast recomputed on the #fbfbfd ground; every step clears
+     the prior cool-gray token's documented ratio. */
+  --ink: #1a1712; /* 17.29:1 — headings, hard marks */
+  --ink-2: #2e2825; /* 14.05:1 — strong text */
+  --body: #423b38; /* 10.61:1 — body text (spec #463f3c was 9.98:1, darkened to clear ≥10:1) */
+  --graphite: #564d48; /* 7.97:1 — labels, values (≥ old 7.72) */
+  --muted: #615750; /* 6.80:1 — secondary mono (≥ old 6.50) */
+  --faint: #6d635b; /* 5.67:1 — non-essential ≥13px (≥ old 5.16) */
+
+  /* hairlines — wine-tinted (rgba of the wine-900 hue), darkening steps */
+  --rule: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.1);
+  --rule-2: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.17);
+  --rule-3: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.26);
+
+  /* THE single accent — burgundy / oxblood, two lightnesses of one hue */
+  --signal: #8a2233; /* fills, dots, rules, marks */
+  --signal-text: #8a2233; /* 8.62:1 on #fbfbfd — small labels / verdicts (≥4.5) */
+  --signal-tint: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.07); /* faint held-row ground */
+  --signal-rule: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.24); /* hairline at signal hue */
 
   /* spacing — 4px base */
   --s1: 4px;
@@ -53,6 +68,33 @@
   --ff-sans: var(--font-sans), system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
   --ff-mono: var(--font-mono), var(--font-geist-mono), ui-monospace, monospace;
 
+  /* ==== OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1, 2026-07-08) — additive site-scoped tokens ==== */
+  /* wine ramp — the ONE brand hue, wine-900 (darkest) → wine-500 */
+  --wine-900: #3e0a13;
+  --wine-800: #55101d;
+  --wine-700: #6e1423;
+  --wine-600: #8a2233;
+  --wine-500: #9e3143;
+  /* accent aliases (the site layer reads these; = the wine ramp) */
+  --accent: #8a2233;
+  --accent-hi: #6e1423; /* hover / pressed */
+  --accent-soft: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.07);
+  --accent-line: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.24);
+  /* severity — kept perceptually FAR from the wine brand (always paired with a
+     shape + word by consuming surfaces, never colour alone) */
+  --error: #d92d20;
+  --error-soft: rgba(217, 45, 32, 0.08);
+  --warn: #b54708;
+  --warn-soft: rgba(181, 71, 8, 0.11);
+  --ok: #067647;
+  --ok-soft: rgba(6, 118, 71, 0.09);
+  /* serif display stack (brand wordmark + headlines) — no new dependency */
+  --serif: ui-serif, "New York", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Palatino, Georgia,
+    "Times New Roman", serif;
+  /* shared easing */
+  --ease: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
+  /* ==== end OXBLOOD SITE LAYER tokens ==== */
+
   font-synthesis: none;
   text-rendering: optimizeLegibility;
   -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
@@ -78,6 +120,121 @@ body {
   -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
 }
 
+/* ============================================================================
+   OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1, 2026-07-08) — shared shell primitives (Nav + footer).
+   Only what the global shell needs; landing/report/demo surfaces keep their own
+   blocks. Consumed by components/Nav.tsx and app/layout.tsx. Element selectors
+   `nav` / `footer` are hidden in the report @media print block (unchanged).
+   ========================================================================== */
+
+/* ----- Nav: sticky, hairline-ruled, serif wordmark, mono links -------------- */
+.site-nav {
+  position: sticky;
+  top: 0;
+  z-index: 40;
+  /* rgba(255,255,255,.88) IS the no-support fallback; blur is progressive */
+  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.88);
+  backdrop-filter: saturate(1.4) blur(10px);
+  -webkit-backdrop-filter: saturate(1.4) blur(10px);
+  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);
+}
+.site-nav-in {
+  max-width: var(--maxw);
+  margin: 0 auto;
+  display: flex;
+  flex-wrap: wrap;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 4px;
+  padding: 11px clamp(18px, 4vw, 36px);
+}
+.site-brand {
+  display: inline-flex;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 9px;
+  margin-right: 18px;
+  font-family: var(--serif);
+  font-weight: 600;
+  letter-spacing: -0.01em;
+  font-size: 16px;
+  color: var(--ink);
+  text-decoration: none;
+}
+.site-brand-mark {
+  width: 22px;
+  height: 22px;
+  flex: none;
+  color: var(--accent);
+}
+.site-navlink {
+  /* mockup spec: sans 13px (E-3 reviewer fix — the dispatch brief's "mono" was
+     the orchestrator's error; the mockup .navlink inherits the sans body font) */
+  font-family: var(--ff-sans);
+  font-size: 13px;
+  color: var(--muted);
+  text-decoration: none;
+  padding: 6px 11px;
+  border-radius: 7px;
+  white-space: nowrap;
+  transition:
+    background 0.16s var(--ease),
+    color 0.16s ease;
+}
+.site-navlink:hover {
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+  color: var(--ink);
+}
+.site-navlink[aria-current="page"] {
+  background: var(--accent);
+  color: #fff;
+}
+.site-navlink:focus-visible,
+.site-brand:focus-visible {
+  outline: 2px solid var(--accent);
+  outline-offset: 2px;
+}
+.site-status {
+  margin-left: auto;
+  display: inline-flex;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 7px;
+  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
+  font-size: 11px;
+  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
+  color: var(--muted);
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  border-radius: 999px;
+  padding: 5px 11px;
+}
+.site-status-pip {
+  width: 6px;
+  height: 6px;
+  border-radius: 50%;
+  background: var(--accent);
+  flex: none;
+}
+
+/* ----- global footer: honesty paragraph on a hairline-ruled panel ----------- */
+.site-footer {
+  margin-top: 20px;
+  border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+}
+.site-footer-in {
+  max-width: var(--maxw);
+  margin: 0 auto;
+  padding: 40px clamp(18px, 4vw, 36px) 72px;
+  font-size: 13px;
+  line-height: 1.62;
+  color: var(--body);
+}
+
+@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+  .site-navlink {
+    transition: none;
+  }
+}
+
 /* ----- type primitives ------------------------------------------------------ */
 
 .ds-mono {

**Verdict: BLOCK**

**Findings**

P2: Root metadata now wraps stale child titles, so the site identity is not unified. [app/layout.tsx](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/layout.tsx:27>) sets `template: "%s · Curbside Commons"`, and installed Next metadata docs say `template` is applied to child titles ([metadata-interface.d.ts](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/node_modules/next/dist/lib/metadata/types/metadata-interface.d.ts:68>); resolver confirms string titles use the stashed template at [resolve-title.js](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/node_modules/next/dist/lib/metadata/resolvers/resolve-title.js:17>)). The landing page still exports `ActivationOps — ...` at [app/page.tsx](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/page.tsx:5>), producing mixed identity in the actual title. It also still renders `ActivationOps` in page content at [app/page.tsx](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/page.tsx:386>). This conflicts with the S1 plan to retire `ActivationOps` and unify to `Curbside Commons` ([docs/plan-whole-site-redesign.md](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/docs/plan-whole-site-redesign.md:7>), [line 32](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/docs/plan-whole-site-redesign.md:32>)).

P3: Comment drift around the reviewer’s nav-font fix. [app/globals.css](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/globals.css:130>) still says “mono links,” but the implemented `.site-navlink` is sans at [line 171](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/globals.css:171>), matching the mockup. Not functional, but it is exactly the kind of stale breadcrumb that can cause a later “fix” to revert the correct sans choice.

**Constraint Checks**

C1 confirmed: `.rpt-wrap {` to EOF is byte-identical to HEAD. SHA-256 matched `3033971e...`; 13,857 bytes in both. Current starts at [app/globals.css](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/globals.css:1262>), HEAD starts at line 1105.

C2 confirmed: no `engine/`, `lib/`, `evals/`, or `fixtures/` files are changed in `git diff --name-only`.

C3 confirmed: footer honesty text nodes are unchanged; the diff only replaces wrapper/style structure in [app/layout.tsx](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/layout.tsx:50>).

C4 confirmed against HEAD for the actual file string: both HEAD and current contain `Prototype · REPLAY · $0.00` ([components/Nav.tsx](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/components/Nav.tsx:57>)). The `/bin/zsh.00` literal is not present.

C5 confirmed: no new `url()`, font CDN, or new `@import`; only pre-existing `@import "tailwindcss"` remains.

C6 confirmed for new motion: `.site-navlink` transition is disabled under `prefers-reduced-motion` at [app/globals.css](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/globals.css:232>); existing global reduced-motion block still disables smooth scroll and reveal transitions at [line 771](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/globals.css:771>).

C7 refuted as an absolute literal claim: `Curbside Commons` also appears in a new CSS comment at [app/globals.css](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/app/globals.css:4>) and in `PLATFORM_NAME` at [lib/product.ts](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/lib/product.ts:13>). Rendered Nav does correctly use `PLATFORM_NAME` at [components/Nav.tsx](</Users/sharan_98/Desktop/AI DoorDash Merchant Engine/components/Nav.tsx:40>).

C8 confirmed: computed contrast on `#FBFBFD` is `--signal-text #8a2233 = 8.62:1` and `--faint #6d635b = 5.67:1`.

C9 confirmed: `package.json` and lockfiles are untouched; no dependency change.

**Validation**

Passed: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `git diff --check`.

Not fully verified: `npm run test` and `npm run test:legacy` could not execute under this read-only sandbox because Vitest failed creating temp SSR dirs with `EPERM: operation not permitted`. `npm run build` was not run because it writes build output.
